Grounding_and_Coping.title: 5-4-3-2-1 grounding
Grounding_and_Coping.message: Pause for a moment. Look around and name 5 things you can see, 4 things you can feel, 3 things you hear, 2 things you smell, and 1 thing you taste. This can help shift your focus away from the urge.

Grounding_and_Coping.title: 10 minute pause
Grounding_and_Coping.message: Give yourself 10 minutes before reacting to the urge. During that time take slow breaths and remind yourself that urges usually pass.

Grounding_and_Coping.title: Change location
Grounding_and_Coping.message: If possible, move to a different place for a few minutes. A short walk or changing rooms can help reset your focus.

Grounding_and_Coping.title: Cold water reset
Grounding_and_Coping.message: Drink a glass of cold water slowly or wash your face with cold water. This small action can help interrupt the urge.

Support_Routing_or_Safe_Space.title: Reach out to someone you trust
Support_Routing_or_Safe_Space.message: Consider calling or messaging a trusted friend or family member and letting them know how you're feeling.

Support_Routing_or_Safe_Space.title: Spend time with supportive people
Support_Routing_or_Safe_Space.message: If possible, spend some time around people who support your recovery goals.

Support_Routing_or_Safe_Space.title: Move to a safer environment
Support_Routing_or_Safe_Space.message: If you're in a place that feels risky, try moving somewhere that feels safer or more comfortable.

Support_Routing_or_Safe_Space.title: Connect with recovery support
Support_Routing_or_Safe_Space.message: If you are part of a recovery group or program, consider checking in with someone from that community.

Coping_Skill_Training.title: Notice the trigger
Coping_Skill_Training.message: Take a moment to think about what may have triggered the urge. Was it stress, a place, or a situation? Recognizing triggers can help you prepare next time.

Coping_Skill_Training.title: Prepare a simple response
Coping_Skill_Training.message: If someone offers substances, you could plan a simple response such as 'No thanks, I'm focusing on other priorities right now.'

Coping_Skill_Training.title: Write down a quick plan
Coping_Skill_Training.message: Write down two or three things you can do when urges appear, such as walking, calling someone, or listening to music.

Coping_Skill_Training.title: Remember a previous success
Coping_Skill_Training.message: Think about a time when you handled a difficult moment successfully. What helped you then?

Routine_and_Progress.title: Plan the next hour
Routine_and_Progress.message: Think about one or two positive activities you can do in the next hour, such as preparing food, exercising, or organizing something small.

Routine_and_Progress.title: Move your body
Routine_and_Progress.message: Try light physical activity like walking, stretching, or simple exercise. Physical movement can help reduce stress.

Routine_and_Progress.title: Recognize your progress
Routine_and_Progress.message: Take a moment to recognize the effort you’ve put into staying on track. Small steps forward still count.

Routine_and_Progress.title: Do something enjoyable
Routine_and_Progress.message: Spend a little time on something you enjoy such as music, reading, cooking, or another hobby.

Motivational_Message.title: Focus on your goals
Motivational_Message.message: Remind yourself why you chose this path. Your goals and future plans are worth protecting.

Motivational_Message.title: Urges pass
Motivational_Message.message: Urges can feel strong, but they usually pass with time. Taking small positive steps can help you get through them.

Motivational_Message.title: Every step matters
Motivational_Message.message: Each time you make a positive choice, you are building stronger habits for the future.

Motivational_Message.title: You’re not alone
Motivational_Message.message: Many people work through similar challenges. Support and understanding are available when you reach out.

Coping_Prompt.title: Three slow breaths
Coping_Prompt.message: Pause and take three slow breaths. Focus only on breathing in and out.

Coping_Prompt.title: Drink water
Coping_Prompt.message: Drink a glass of water slowly and take a short pause.

Coping_Prompt.title: Step outside briefly
Coping_Prompt.message: If possible, step outside or look out a window for a few minutes.

Coping_Prompt.title: Send a quick message
Coping_Prompt.message: Consider sending a short message to someone supportive just to stay connected.
